what_it_does: A human-readable, chronological release ledger for the episodic-memory project, organized by Semantic Versioning release numbers and grouped into Added, Changed, Fixed, Removed, Security, and Documentation sections.
observations: Follows the Keep a Changelog format and claims to adhere to Semantic Versioning.
Covers releases from 1.0.0 through 1.4.2, newest first.
Contains extensive bug-fix narratives, especially around sync queue management, summarization, MCP wrapper health, and Windows support.
Major subsystems mentioned include Claude Code integration, Codex plugin support, embedding search, SQLite indexing, and MCP server tooling.
Many entries credit contributors by GitHub handle and reference issue/PR numbers.
Includes operational guidance such as migration batch sizes, rollback safety, environment variables, and kill-lingering-process instructions.
Has dedicated sections for security, documentation, and removed features such as the /search-conversations slash command.
standouts: Critical recursive process explosion fix in 1.1.2 using a reentrancy guard environment variable.
Embedding model upgrade to bge-small-en-v1.5 in 1.2.0 with automatic background migration and rollback safety.
Native Codex plugin support and cross-harness recall added in 1.3.0.
Detailed 1.4.2 fixes for multi-project sync crashes, ChatGPT Codex model suffix errors, and concurrent sync worker races.
Version-consistency automation introduced in 1.1.1 with a single source of truth and drift tests.
conventions: Uses standard Keep a Changelog headings per release: Added, Changed, Fixed, Removed, Security, Documentation.
Versions follow Semantic Versioning and each release has an ISO-like date.
References issues, PRs, and contributor handles inline.
Occasionally includes narrative sub-sections with performance numbers and migration instructions.
Uses future calendar dates (2026), which is atypical for a real-world changelog at current knowledge cutoff.
concerns: Some bullets bundle multiple distinct fixes or long causal stories, making atomic scanning harder.
Future-dated releases (2026) may be synthetic or planned rather than historical.
Contains interpretive postmortems (e.g., misidentified npm hoisting bug) that may not be independently verifiable from the changelog alone.
No explicit Deprecated section is used, despite the format supporting it.
Severity or priority of fixes is implied by narrative length rather than a structured label.
notes: This is the episodic-memory project's release changelog: a chronological, versioned summary of fixes, features, migrations, and operational notes from 1.0.0 through 1.4.2.
questions: Are the 2026 release dates actual historical dates, planned future releases, or synthetic placeholders?
Which commits or tags correspond to each PR/fix listed?
How is 'notable' defined when deciding what to include?
What is the exact relationship between the Claude Code and Codex support described and the underlying code changes?
Is there an automated process ensuring every merged change appears in this file?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file documents notable versioned changes in Keep a Changelog format, including added features, fixed bugs, changed behavior, removed commands, security notes, and migration guidance.
apparent_themes: Operational reliability and graceful failure handling
Incremental search and memory quality improvements
intention_signals: To give users and maintainers a trustworthy, readable history that justifies upgrades, highlights critical fixes, explains migrations, and shows responsive maintenance to bug reports.
novelty: It blends conventional release notes with detailed operational postmortems, environment-variable knobs, and runbook-style troubleshooting guidance rather than terse one-line changes.
contribution: It contributes a curated, versioned, human-readable record of product evolution, user-impact statements, migration instructions, and contributor/issue traceability that helps users, maintainers, and support understand what changed and why.
summary_for_embedding: CHANGELOG.md is a Keep-a-Changelog-formatted release history for the episodic-memory project, listing semantic-versioned releases from 1.0.0 to 1.4.2 with added features, fixed bugs, changed behavior, removed commands, security notes, and migration guidance.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to communicate notable changes, upgrade rationale, and operational impact to users and maintainers across releases.
